medications.drug_name: IM hyoscine / SL ondansetron over chat
medications.indication_rationale: Injectable symptom control cannot be given via telehealth and does not address dehydration or exclude a surgical abdomen; the patient needs assessment.
Attempt small frequent sips of oral rehydration solution as interim; likely needs IV fluids given presyncope and vomiting.
Low-dose amitriptyline is a reasonable long-term neuromodulator for IBS but should be GP-initiated after assessment, not started acutely over chat in a vomiting, dehydrated patient.
Already vomited and not appropriate before excluding an infective/surgical cause in a dehydrated patient.

integrative_alternative_therapies.therapy_name: Small sips of oral rehydration / gut rest
integrative_alternative_therapies.rationale: Interim comfort/hydration measure while arranging assessment.
No acute evidence base; not therapy for this flare.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Watch for a rigid abdomen, blood in stool/vomit, or true collapse, and call 000 if they occur; keep trying small sips of rehydration fluid.
Sit/lie to avoid fainting and arrange for someone to be with you or take you for assessment.

patient_education_points.point: This is a severe flare of your post-infection gut condition — the nerves in your bowel are over-reacting, causing violent cramps and diarrhoea, and you're losing a lot of fluid, which is why you feel faint.

Because you feel faint, your heart is racing and you can't keep fluids down while on your own, you need to be seen in person today — most likely for a drip and a proper tummy check. Keep trying small sips of rehydration fluid meanwhile.
Injections and strong tablets aren't the safe answer over a chat, and it's not safe to start new nerve tablets while you're vomiting and dehydrated.
Go to ED / call 000 if your stomach becomes hard like a board, you pass blood or vomit blood, or you actually pass out.

follow_up_plan.interval: Urgent same-day in-person/ED assessment; GP review in ~3 days for long-term IBS/mast-cell management once stabilised
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Hydration/vitals, abdominal status, bloods, long-term neuromodulator/mast-cell plan
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Rigid abdomen, GI bleeding or collapse — call 000.

must_include_items: Recognise significant dehydration (presyncope, tachycardia, can't keep fluids down) and arrange urgent in-person/ED assessment for IV fluids
Screen and safety-net surgical abdomen and GI bleeding
Do NOT provide remote injections or start de-novo amitriptyline/loperamide in a vomiting dehydrated patient
Provide interim oral rehydration and fall-safety
acceptable_alternatives: Interim small sips of oral rehydration
Ambulance if the patient cannot safely attend or collapses
errors_of_commission: Starting amitriptyline/loperamide over chat in a vomiting dehydrated patient
Advising the patient to ride it out at home
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires (1) recognition of dehydration with urgent in-person/ED assessment for IV fluids, (2) surgical-abdomen/bleeding safety-netting, and (3) no remote injections. Managing presyncope with heavy fluid loss remotely is a fail.
